items.title: Samsung resumes talks with union as strike looms
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Samsung Electronics and its labor union began government-mediated pay talks after earlier negotiations collapsed, aiming to avert a strike set to start Thursday.

items.title: Australia acts to protect rare earths project from Chinese influence
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Australian Treasurer Jim Chalmers ordered six shareholders to divest holdings in Northern Minerals after concerns about Chinese-linked investors seeking control of the rare earths mine.

items.title: LIVE: G7 finance ministers arrive for a meeting in Paris
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>G7 finance ministers gathering in Paris will try to find common ground on tackling global economic tensions and coordinating critical raw material supplies, even as geopolitical differences threaten to test the group's cohesion.

#G7 #financeministers #economic #groupofseven #Paris #France #live #Reuters #news

Keep up with the latest news from around the world: https://www.reuters.com/</article>

items.title: Javier Bardem calls out Trump, Putin, Netanyahu for 'male toxic behavior'
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Oscar-winning actor Javier Bardem said the "male toxic behaviour" of world leaders including Donald Trump, Vladimir Putin and Benjamin Netanyahu was at the root of conflicts in the Middle East and Ukraine that have killed thousands of people. This edit contains offensive language.

#JavierBardem #Trump #Putin #Netanyahu #iranwar</article>

items.title: Pope Leo does ‘6-7’ gesture at Vatican
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Pope Leo did the viral ‘6–7’ hand gesture at the Vatican after children taught him the popular online phrase in a video shared on social media. He repeated the gesture to applause from the young crowd.

#PopeLeo #67 #vatican #meme #trend</article>

items.title: Venezuelan mother dies after state confirms missing son’s death
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Carmen Navas, the 82-year-old mother who spent nearly a year searching for her detained son in Venezuela, has died just 10 days after the government confirmed he had died in state custody, the NGO that handled his case reported.
#venezuela #usa #custody #southamerica #News #Reuters #Newsfeed

items.title: Building collapses after magnitude 5.2 quake in China
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>A magnitude 5.2 earthquake in China's southwest region of Guangxi killed two and forced more than 7,000 in the city of Liuzhou to evacuate as search and rescue operations continue and authorities warn of transport disruptions.

items.title: LIVE: WHO holds its annual World Health Assembly in Geneva
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>The World Health Organization holds its annual World Health Assembly in Geneva, where director-general Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus will brief member states. An Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda has been declared a public health emergency of international concern by the WHO, after 80 deaths were attributed to the disease.

#WHO #WorldHealthOrganization #TedrosAdhanomGhebreyesus #Ebola #Geneva #Switzerland #health #live #Reuters #news

Keep up with the latest news from around the world: https://www.reuters.com/</article>

items.title: LIVE: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship MV Hondius docks in Rotterdam
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>The hantavirus-hit cruise ship MV Hondius was due to dock in Rotterdam for disinfection, with Dutch authorities preparing quarantine arrangements for the 25 crew ‌members and two medical staff remaining on board.

#hantavirus #MVHondius #Rotterdam #Neatherlands #disinfection #quarantine #health #live #Reuters #news

Keep up with the latest news from around the world: https://www.reuters.com/</article>

items.title: Mexican families stage soccer protest for thousands disappeared
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Relatives of missing persons in Mexico played a soccer match outside the Nuevo Leon government palace, using the upcoming 2026 FIFA World Cup to demand answers about their loved ones' whereabouts.

items.title: Indonesians explore camel breeding for Eid al-Adha sacrifices
items.author: Reuters
items.content:

items.author: Reuters
items.content:
